title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The UK government has published its response to a report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards. The response outlines the government's plans and intentions for implementing the recommendations made in the original report.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This policy document assesses the impact of using copyrighted works in artificial intelligence systems. It aims to provide insights into the copyright implications of AI development.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education's AI policy document addresses the use of generative AI in the education sector. It outlines the department's position on how generative AI should be utilized to support teaching and learning.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport Artificial Intelligence Action Plan is a government document outlining how to benefit from AI technology in the transport sector while minimizing potential risks. The plan involves working with the transport sector to unlock the possibilities of AI and make informed decisions about its development.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This report discusses the use of copyrighted material in the development of AI systems and outlines progress made under specific laws. It provides an update on the implementation of regulations related to copyright and AI.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is responding to the Regulatory Horizons Council's recommendations to regulate artificial intelligence used in medical devices. This response aims to ensure the safe and effective use of AI in medical settings.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's plans for regulating the use of artificial intelligence in qualification assessments. It was developed in response to government requests for guidance on this issue.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has issued a response to a report by the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence, acknowledging its findings and outlining steps to address concerns about AI in the UK. The response aims to provide guidance on implementing the recommended changes to ensure the UK effectively manages the impact of artificial intelligence.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the scope and purpose of a committee tasked with reviewing the intersection of artificial intelligence and public standards. It provides terms of reference and answers frequently asked questions about the review process.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Centre for Data Ethics and Innovation's partner, the Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL), has given written advice to a government committee about how to control artificial intelligence. This submission is part of the committee's wider investigation into AI rules.
